legalCitations.act: Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), 15 U.S.C. § 1692 et seq.
legalCitations.description: A debt collector may contact third parties only to locate the consumer, may not state that the consumer owes any debt, and may not contact a third party more than once unless requested by the third party or if the collector believes the earlier response was erroneous.

legalCitations.act: Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), 15 U.S.C. § 1692 et seq.
legalCitations.description: Except as provided in § 1692b (location information), a debt collector may not communicate with any person other than the consumer, the consumer's attorney, a consumer reporting agency, the creditor, or the creditor's attorney in connection with collection of a debt.

legalCitations.act: Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), 15 U.S.C. § 1692 et seq.
legalCitations.description: A debt collector may not engage in conduct the natural consequence of which is to harass, oppress, or abuse any person in connection with the collection of a debt.

subject: FORMAL NOTICE — CEASE ALL CONTACT WITH THIRD PARTIES REGARDING THIS ALLEGED DEBT
openingParagraph: I am writing to formally demand that you immediately cease and desist all contact with any third parties — including but not limited to family members, neighbors, friends, or coworkers — regarding the above-referenced alleged debt. Such contact is a direct violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), 15 U.S.C. §§ 1692b and 1692c(b).
bodyParagraphs: Under 15 U.S.C. § 1692c(b), debt collectors are prohibited from communicating about a consumer's debt with third parties except in extremely narrow circumstances (obtaining location information, communicating with a consumer's attorney, or communicating with a consumer reporting agency). Contacting family members, neighbors, or other individuals about my debt violates this provision.

Even the limited exception for location information (15 U.S.C. § 1692b) prohibits a collector from disclosing that a consumer owes a debt, from contacting the same third party more than once, or from engaging in any conduct that could reasonably be construed as harassing or embarrassing the consumer.

Contacting people in my life about this alleged debt is not only a violation of federal law, it is causing me serious emotional distress and potential reputational harm. I am documenting all third-party contacts from your organization, including the identities of the individuals contacted and the content of those communications.

I am fully aware of my right under 15 U.S.C. § 1692k to sue for actual damages, statutory damages of up to $1,000, and attorney's fees for each FDCPA violation. Third-party contact is one of the most serious violations under the FDCPA and often results in significant actual damage awards.
demands: Immediately cease all contact with any and all third parties regarding this alleged debt
Specifically cease all contact with my family members, neighbors, friends, and coworkers
Contact only me directly at the address listed in this letter
Provide written confirmation that all third-party contact has ceased
Provide the names of all third parties you have contacted and the content of those communications
consequencesStatement: Any further third-party contact after receipt of this letter will be reported to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), and my state attorney general. I will also pursue civil litigation under 15 U.S.C. § 1692k for actual damages — which can be substantial when third-party contact causes reputational harm, damaged relationships, or emotional distress — plus statutory damages of $1,000 and attorney's fees.
closingStatement: This is your formal written notice to immediately cease all third-party contact. Respond in writing to confirm compliance. Do not telephone me or any person in my life regarding this matter.
